text:
The inputs of efficiency include a sense of 
belonging and of accountability which springs in 
the bosom of the bureaucracy (not pejoratively 
used) if its composition takes in also the weaker 
segments of ‘We, the people of India’. No other 
understanding can reconcile the claim of the 
radical present and the hangover of the unjust 
past.”
833. A similar view was expressed in Vasanth Kumar [1985 
Supp SCC 714 : 1985 Supp 1 SCR 352] by Chinnappa 
Reddy, J. The learned Judge said (SCC p. 739, para 36)
“[T]he mere securing of high marks at an 
examination may not necessarily mark out a 
good administrator. An efficient administrator, 
one takes it, must be one who possesses among 
other qualities the capacity to understand with 
sympathy and, therefore, to tackle bravely the 
problems of a large segment of population 
constituting the weaker sections of the people. 
310 
Supreme Court Reports
And, who better than the ones belonging to 
those very sections? Why not ask ourselves
